questions.question: What is the minimum age to vote in India?
questions.explanation: The voting age was lowered from 21 to 18 by the 61st Amendment Act, 1988.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the CEC and other Election Commissioners.

questions.question: What does EPIC stand for in the context of elections?
questions.explanation: EPIC is the official term for your Voter ID card.

questions.question: How often are General Elections held in India?
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha elections are held every 5 years unless dissolved earlier.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution provides for the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 provides for the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections.

questions.question: What is the maximum limit of candidates per EVM?
questions.explanation: A single EVM can record a maximum of 64 candidates (including NOTA) using multiple balloting units.

questions.question: In which year were EVMs first used in India on a large scale?
questions.explanation: EVMs were used in all 543 constituencies for the first time in 2004.

questions.question: What is the ink used to mark a voter's finger called?
questions.explanation: Indelible ink contains silver nitrate which reacts with skin to leave a semi-permanent mark.

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen served as the first CEC from 1950 to 1958.

questions.question: What is the 'None of the Above' (NOTA) option?
questions.explanation: NOTA allows voters to officially register their disapproval of all candidates.

questions.question: What is the VVPAT system used for?
questions.explanation: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail allows voters to verify that their vote was cast correctly.

questions.question: Which state was the first to use EVMs in an assembly election?
questions.explanation: EVMs were first used in 50 polling stations of Paravur constituency in Kerala in 1982.

questions.question: What is the Model Code of Conduct (MCC)?
questions.explanation: MCC is a set of guidelines issued by the ECI for conduct of political parties and candidates.

questions.question: How many constituencies are there in the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: India is divided into 543 Parliamentary constituencies for Lok Sabha elections.

questions.question: What is the tenure of the Chief Election Commissioner?
questions.explanation: The CEC holds office for 6 years or until they attain the age of 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: Can a person with a criminal record vote?
questions.explanation: Under Section 62(5) of RPA, people in prison cannot vote, but those on bail can.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of days for an election campaign?
questions.explanation: Campaigning must stop 48 hours before the conclusion of the poll.

questions.question: Who is eligible to be a voter in India?
questions.explanation: Every citizen over 18, unless disqualified, has the right to be registered as a voter.

questions.question: What is the symbol of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: The ECI uses the Lion Capital of Ashoka as its official emblem.

questions.question: Which app allows users to report MCC violations?
questions.explanation: cVIGIL stands for Vigilant Citizen and allows reporting of election misconduct.

quests.title: The First Vote
quests.description: Learn about the first general election of 1951.

quests.title: ID Master
quests.description: Verify your name in the electoral roll.

quests.title: Democracy Shield
quests.description: Read about the Model Code of Conduct.
